title: Camera System Health
dataSources: Video management system logs (camera status, recording status)
spl:
index=physical sourcetype="vms:camera_status"
| where recording_status!="recording" OR connection_status!="connected"
| table camera_id, location, connection_status, recording_status, last_frame
description: Offline cameras create security blind spots. Monitoring ensures continuous surveillance coverage.
value: Offline cameras create security blind spots. Monitoring ensures continuous surveillance coverage.
grandmaExplanation: Offline cameras create security blind spots — so power, cooling, and physical access problems are found before they hurt uptime.
implementation: Poll camera/NVR status via API or forward VMS events. Alert on camera offline, recording failure, or storage issues. Track camera uptime percentage. Report on coverage gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: NVR/VMS syslog or API.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Video management system logs (camera status, recording status).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Poll camera/NVR status via API or forward VMS events. Alert on camera offline, recording failure, or storage issues. Track camera uptime percentage. Report on coverage gaps.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="vms:camera_status"
| where recording_status!="recording" OR connection_status!="connected"
| table camera_id, location, connection_status, recording_status, last_frame
```

Understanding this SPL

**Camera System Health** — Offline cameras create security blind spots. Monitoring ensures continuous surveillance coverage.

Documented **Data sources**: Video management system logs (camera status, recording status). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): NVR/VMS syslog or API. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: physical; **sourcetype**: vms:camera_status. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=physical, sourcetype="vms:camera_status". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where recording_status!="recording" OR connection_status!="connected"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Camera System Health**): table camera_id, location, connection_status, recording_status, last_frame


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status grid (camera × status), Table (offline cameras), Single value (cameras recording %), Floor plan (camera locations with status).
visualization: Status grid (camera × status), Table (offline cameras), Single value (cameras recording %), Floor plan (camera locations with status).
